Troubleshooting

This page covers the most common problems encountered when running Merlin. For installation-related issues, also check Installation and Configuration.

Invoices not processing

If uploaded invoices stay in a processing state or fail immediately, check the following in order:

  1. Magika binary — run magika --version in a terminal. If the command is not found, install Magika and make sure it is on the system path, or set MAGIKA_BINARY in .env to its full path.
  2. Queue worker — if QUEUE_CONNECTION=database, a queue worker must be running. Either switch to QUEUE_CONNECTION=sync (recommended for local development) or start a worker with php artisan queue:listen.
  3. Anthropic API key — verify that ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set in .env and is valid. Test by looking at recent LLM log entries in Settings › LLM Logs for error messages.
  4. Watch folder command — if using the watch folder rather than manual upload, confirm that php artisan invoices:watch is running. Without it, files dropped into the watch folder are never picked up.
  5. Log output — check storage/logs/laravel.log for exception details.

Vite manifest errors

Symptom: Every page shows an error like "Unable to locate file in Vite manifest".

Cause: The Vite asset build has not been run, so the compiled CSS and JavaScript files do not exist.

Fix: Run npm run build to build the production assets, or run npm run dev (or composer run dev) to start the Vite development server. The error will disappear as soon as a valid manifest file exists.

Queued jobs never run

Symptom: Invoice uploads appear to complete, but no processing happens. Invoice status stays as uploaded indefinitely. Emails are never sent.

Cause: QUEUE_CONNECTION=database is set but no queue worker is running. Jobs are inserted into the jobs table but nothing is consuming them.

Fix for local development: Set QUEUE_CONNECTION=sync in .env. With sync, jobs run inline immediately on dispatch — no worker is needed.

Fix for production: Start and keep running php artisan queue:listen --tries=1 (or use a process supervisor such as Supervisor to manage it). The composer run dev script already starts a queue worker alongside the other development processes.

Model not found (404 from Claude)

Symptom: Invoice processing fails with an error mentioning not_found_error or a 404 response from the Anthropic API. Alert emails arrive at ANTHROPIC_ALERT_RECIPIENTS.

Cause: One of the Claude model IDs in .env (ANTHROPIC_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_MODEL_FAST, or ANTHROPIC_MODEL_BACKUP) refers to a model that Anthropic has retired. The model health ladder will escalate to the next available model automatically, but the root cause must be fixed to restore normal operation.

Fix: Update the affected ANTHROPIC_MODEL* variable in .env to a current model alias. Use the aliases listed in Configuration — Anthropic API rather than dated snapshot IDs where possible. Restart the application after changing .env.

Permission denied or missing menu items

Symptom: A user receives an "unauthorised" error when accessing a page, or menu items that should be visible are missing.

Cause: The user's role does not include the required permission for that page or action. Menu items in Merlin are hidden when the logged-in user lacks the permission to access them — this is by design, not a bug.

Fix:

  1. Go to Settings › Roles and find the role assigned to the affected user.
  2. Verify that the role includes the permissions the user needs (see Roles & Permissions for the full permission list).
  3. If the role is missing a permission, add it and save.
  4. If the user has no role at all, go to Settings › Users and assign a role.

Authorisation in Merlin always uses $user->can('permission-name'). Checking role names (hasRole()) is never used anywhere in the application.

Foreign-currency rates not updating

Symptom: Foreign-currency invoices always show the same exchange rate, even after many days have passed.

Causes and fixes:

  • Missing API key — check that EXCHANGERATE_API_KEY is set in .env. Without it, the exchange rate service cannot fetch rates.
  • Cache TTL — exchange rates are cached for 24 hours (86 400 seconds). This is expected behaviour; rates will update once per day. If you need to force an immediate update, clear the application cache with php artisan cache:clear.
  • API error — check storage/logs/laravel.log for errors from the ExchangeRate-API. The API may be unreachable or your key may have expired.
